---
title: "The Tourism Scissors"
---

# Project Description

This project explores the "Tourism Scissors" effect in Turkey (2015–2026), a striking divergence where domestic travel declines as international tourism surges. By merging monthly aviation mobility data from DHMİ with key macroeconomic indicators (USD/TRY exchange rates and CPI) from the Central Bank of Turkey (TCMB), we aim to decode the intricate relationship between economic shifts and travel behavior. Our analysis pinpoints critical inflection points and tracks evolving passenger dynamics across major tourism hubs like Antalya, Muğla, and İzmir.

### Team Members

-   [Ayşenaz Özyer](https://emu-hacettepe-analytics.github.io/emu430-spring2026-aysenazozyer/)
-   [Başar Yılmaz](https://emu-hacettepe-analytics.github.io/emu430-spring2026-BasarYiilmaz/)
-   [Fatma Şeyma Toksöz](https://emu-hacettepe-analytics.github.io/emu430-spring2026-b2220469019/)
-   [Sude Nakişci](https://emu-hacettepe-analytics.github.io/emu430-spring2026-sudenakisci/)

### Key Takeaways

-   **Core Focus**: Examines the divergence between domestic and international flight demand in Turkey, termed the "Tourism Scissors" effect.

-   **Data Integration**: Utilizes a dual-source dataset combining DHMİ passenger statistics with TCMB macroeconomic indicators (exchange rates and CPI) from 2015 to 2026.

-   **Regional Analysis**: Specifically targets major tourism hubs (Antalya, Muğla, İzmir) to measure the localized impact of economic changes on travel behavior.
